bead.title: Delete legacy direct agent execution transcript handling
bead.description:
PROBLEM
DDx still contains a legacy direct Claude stream-json execution/transcript path. After DDx runs agent work through Fizeau, this alternate code path violates the boundary and can produce divergent transcript semantics from Fizeau.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go contains runClaudeStreamingFn, parseClaudeStream, claudeStreamArgsUnsupported, and Fizeau-style event synthesis from Claude stream-json.
- cli/internal/agent/runner.go and tests still allow DDX_USE_NEW_AGENT_PATH / legacy fallback routing to select DDx-owned parser behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
Delete the DDx-owned Claude stream parser and any execution fallback that can route transcript-producing agent work through it. DDx should invoke Fizeau for agent execution and consume Fizeau events/results through the opaque/final-result adapter.

NON-SCOPE
- DDx final Result projection from Fizeau final events.
- Fizeau harness internals.
- Historical artifact readability for already-created logs.
bead.acceptance:
1. Production code has no DDx-owned Claude stream-json parser; rg -n 'runClaudeStreamingFn|parseClaudeStream|claudeStreamArgsUnsupported|DDX_USE_NEW_AGENT_PATH' cli/internal/agent cli/cmd returns no production hits.
2. Production code no longer synthesizes Fizeau/internal transcript events such as tool.call/tool.result from Claude stream-json.
3. Tests that covered the deleted parser are removed or rewritten around the Fizeau service adapter boundary.
4. TestAgentExecution_UsesFizeauServicePathOnly or equivalent proves transcript-producing agent work cannot select the deleted direct parser.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'Test.*Claude|Test.*Agent|Test.*Service|Test.*Fizeau' -count=1 passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

governing.title: Feature: DDx Agent Service (consumer of ddx-agent contract)
